name: DNS Information Leakage (HINFO, TXT, LOC, RP Records)
description: Detects excessive information disclosure through DNS resource records that reveal internal infrastructure details, operating systems, physical locations, or responsible persons. Per NIST SP 800-81r3 Section 3.5, organizations should minimize public-facing record content and implement split-horizon DNS to separate internal and external views.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Remove unnecessary HINFO, LOC, and RP records from public-facing DNS zones. Audit TXT records for sensitive information disclosure. Implement split-horizon DNS to separate internal and external DNS views. Minimize the information exposed in public DNS records. Review SPF, DKIM, and DMARC TXT records for overly permissive configurations.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: DNS information leakage provides attackers with infrastructure mapping data during the reconnaissance phase. HINFO records revealing OS types and LOC records revealing physical locations have been used in targeted attacks.
